description: Provide a migration entry point that detects older `schema_version` values and applies registered migrations in order. `Initialize-SlashOps` calls this when a known older version is found; unknown future versions are refused without modification, and a `migration_log` entry is written into the migrated config (or history file) recording the bump.
notes: v0.1 ships only schema `"1"` — the dispatcher exists to make later bumps mechanical.
steps: TDD: Old schema version (e.g. `"0"`) triggers migration to `"1"` after backup.
TDD: Unknown future schema (e.g. `"99"`) is left untouched and returns refusal.
TDD: Migration creates a backup before writing (cfg-05).
TDD: `migration_log` entry recorded with timestamp, from-version, to-version.
TDD: Migration is idempotent — re-running on a `"1"` file is a no-op.
Author `Private/Config/Invoke-ConfigMigration.ps1` taking `(Path)`, dispatching to a registered migration map keyed by from-version.
Each migration is a small private function — register them in a hashtable so future versions can be added without touching the dispatcher.
name: [cfg-10] Config migration framework
